message:
perf(server,cli): suppress noisy fsqlite tracing, relocate integrity check, and minimize worker pool allocations

Logging filter (cli/lib.rs, main.rs): Replace the blanket "info" default
tracing filter with a targeted filter that suppresses high-frequency
fsqlite internal spans at warn level: fsqlite_core::connection,
fsqlite_mvcc::{observability,gc,rebase}, fsqlite_wal::checkpoint_executor,
and fsqlite_vdbe::{jit,engine}. This eliminates hundreds of per-query
tracing events that previously flooded stderr during normal operation.
Both the CLI (apply_release_logging_defaults) and MCP server (main.rs)
now share the same filter list via dedicated default_*_log_filter()
functions. Also directs the MCP server's tracing subscriber to stderr
explicitly and disables target display for cleaner output.

Startup integrity check relocation (server/lib.rs, startup_checks.rs):
Move the probe_integrity() call out of run_startup_probes() and into
readiness_check(), which already creates a single-connection pool and
validates the database. This consolidates all pre-listen database
validation into one function, avoids creating a redundant pool just for
the integrity probe, and fixes a subtle issue where
note_startup_integrity_probe_completed() was called unconditionally even
when config.integrity_check_on_startup was false. The startup probes
test expectation is updated from 7 to 6 critical probes, and the now
call-site-only probe_integrity/attempt_probe_recovery functions get
#[allow(dead_code)] annotations since they remain useful for manual or
future automated recovery paths.

Worker pool minimization (ack_ttl.rs, cleanup.rs, integrity_guard.rs):
Configure the ack-TTL scanner, cleanup worker, and integrity guard
background threads to create minimal 1-connection pools (min=1, max=1,
warmup=0) instead of inheriting the default pool sizing from
DbPoolConfig::from_env(). The ack-TTL and cleanup workers also now
explicitly clone the database URL from config and skip migrations, since
readiness_check() already ran migrations before these workers start.
The integrity guard already had url cloning but now also gets the
min/max/warmup reduction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
